33. Insofar as the judgment in the case of Satish Batra 
(supra) is concerned, the clause providing for “forfeiture of 
earnest money deposit” cannot be said to be one-sided. […]
34. It can thus be seen that in the aforesaid case though 
the term in the Agreement provided for forfeiture of the 
earnest money in the event the prospective purchaser 
fails to fulfill the conditions, it also provided for payment 
of double the amount of earnest money by the vendor to 
the purchaser in case the vendor fails to complete the 
transaction. As such, the said term cannot be said to be 
one-sided.”
(Emphasis supplied)
51. On a conspectus of the aforementioned authorities, it is evident 
that a clause for the forfeiture of earnest money is not penal in the 
ordinary sense, rendering Section 74 of the 1872 Act, inapplicable.
